Patch Set 2:

Sorry I didn't reply earlier but we still need to fix this. Without security_file_alloc() this code will crash. What we really need to do is move to alloc_file_pseudo(). The only reason I didn't is due to alloc_file_pseudo() also allocating a pseudo dentry. In the case of osd_ios_general_scan() we supply a dentry. The others look like we could  just use the default pseudo dentries.

Patch Set 2:

Arrg.  That "quasi-file" stuff in osd-ldiskfs really need to be re-written.  I'll see what I can come up with.

parents.subject: LU-13783 libcfs: support absence of account_page_dirtied

commit.subject: LU-13783 osd: only lookup security_file_alloc if possible.
commit.message:
LU-13783 osd: only lookup security_file_alloc if possible.

Some kernels don't export kallsyms_lookup_name().  On these kernels we
mustn't even try to lookup security_file_alloc().  We must just
survive without it.

Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: If0652d0e466fc831dafb6d4de7dc61b9ffc0944d
